sections.paragraph:
ἀποστολή, ῆς, ἡ (s. ἀποστέλλω; Eur., Thu., et al. in var. mngs.; Diod S 36, 1 [ἀ. στρατιωτῶν=‘sending out’ of troops]; ins, pap, LXX; TestNapht 2:1; EpArist 15; Jos., Ant. 20, 50, Vi. 268) in our lit. only of God’s elite emissaries for the Christian message office of a special emissary, apostleship, office of an apostle, assignment w. διακονία Ac 1:25. Used esp. by Paul to designate his position: ἡ σφραγίς μου τ. ἀποστολῆς the seal (official confirmation) of my apostleship 1 Cor 9:2. ἐνεργεῖν τινι εἰς ἀ. make someone capable of being an apostle Gal 2:8. λαμβάνειν ἀποστολὴν εἰς ὑπακοὴν πίστεως receive apostleship, to bring about obedience that is consonant with faith Ro 1:5.—DELG s.v. στέλλω A. M-M. TW.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

var. var. = various(ly)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestNapht TestNapht = Testament of Naphthali, s. Test12Patr—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

esp. esp. = especially

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 121
